mnemonics.title: SONAME Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: SONAME = SO + NAME (SO-NAME) - contains Major version number that programs link against. Think: 'SOunds Right with NAME and version'.

mnemonics.title: ldconfig Steps
mnemonics.content: ldconfig = Link-Direct-Config: 1) Reads /etc/ld.so.conf 2) Scans library directories 3) Creates symbolic links 4) Updates /etc/ld.so.cache

acronyms.usage: Code that can execute from any memory address without modification, required for shared libraries
The name embedded in library containing major version (e.g., libfoo.so.1)
API for dynamic library loading at runtime (dlopen, dlsym, dlclose)
Windows equivalent of Linux shared libraries (.so files)
The dynamic linker/loader (ld.so) that loads shared libraries at runtime

visualTips.visualization: Draw three boxes: Linker Name (libfoo.so) → SONAME (libfoo.so.1) → Real Name (libfoo.so.1.0.0). Think of it as a chain: start simple, add version, add full version.
Draw a hierarchy: 1) LD_PRELOAD (top priority) 2) LD_LIBRARY_PATH 3) /etc/ld.so.cache 4) Default paths (/lib, /usr/lib). Like checking VIP list first, then your own list, then system list.
Static: Copy paste entire book into your notes. Shared: Reference the library book everyone shares - saves paper (disk space) and everyone reads same copy (memory).

analogies.analogy: Like a community library - books (code) are not copied into every home (executable), but everyone accesses the same shared resource, saving space and allowing updates without re-printing.
Like ordering food at a restaurant - you don't cook everything yourself (static), you request what you need when you need it (lazy binding), and the kitchen (library) prepares it on demand.
Like a phone book - program has a name (symbol), dynamic linker looks up the address (memory location) in the library to connect the call.
